title: Bino Report Bundle
description: Configuration documents used by bino build to collect data and render reports.

apiVersion.description: API version of the report bundle. Must always be 'bino.bi/v1alpha1' for this schema.

kind.description: Type of document in the report bundle (e.g. DataSource, DataSet, LayoutPage). Determines which spec block is expected.

metadata.description: Standard object metadata used to name and label this document. The name is used by other documents to reference this one.

spec.description: Kind-specific configuration payload. Its structure depends on the value of 'kind' (for example dataSourceSpec for kind=DataSource).

name.description: Name of this document. Must be unique per kind within each ReportArtefact after applying constraints. Used for references from other manifests (for example a DataSet query or LayoutPage child). Allowed characters: letters, digits, hyphen and underscore (e.g. 'coffee_sales_daily').

labels.description: Key-value pairs for categorization. ReportArtefact labels are used as context when evaluating constraints on other documents.

annotations.description: Key-value pairs for arbitrary metadata. Not used by the system.

oneOf.description: String format constraint: 'field operator value', e.g., 'mode==preview', 'labels.env in [dev,staging]'

operator.description: Comparison operator: '==' (equals), '!=' (not equals), 'in' (value in array), 'not-in' (value not in array)

value.description: Value to compare: string or boolean for ==, !=; string array for in, not-in

oneOf.description: Structured format constraint with field, operator, and value

constraints.description: Constraint expressions that determine if this document is included for a specific ReportArtefact. Each constraint is evaluated against the target artefact's labels, spec, and the current mode (build/preview/serve). All constraints must match for inclusion. Supports both string format ('mode==preview', 'labels.env in [dev,staging]') and structured format ({field: 'mode', operator: '==', value: 'preview'}). Not applicable to ReportArtefact documents.

type.description: Kind of asset to register: 'image' for logos and pictures, 'file' for generic binary files, 'font' for custom fonts used in layouts.

mediaType.description: MIME media type of the asset (for example 'image/png', 'image/svg+xml', 'font/woff2'). Used when embedding the asset into the report.

purpose.description: Optional free-form description of how this asset is used (for example 'brand-logo', 'heading-font').

source.description: Where to load the asset from (inline base64, local file path, or remote URL). Exactly one source variant must be provided.

inlineBase64.description: Base64-encoded asset content embedded directly in the manifest. Useful for fully self-contained bundles.

localPath.description: Relative or absolute path to a local asset file (for example './assets/logo.png'). Resolved relative to the manifest file.

remoteURL.description: HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to a remotely hosted asset (for example 'https://example.com/logo.svg').

sqlIdentifier.description: Name of the DuckDB view created from this DataSource. Must be a valid unquoted SQL identifier in lowercase (letter or underscore followed by lowercase letters, digits, or underscores), for example 'coffee_sales_daily'. Use this name in DataSet queries.

dataFileFormat.description: File format of input data files. Choose 'csv', 'excel', 'parquet' or 'json' so DuckDB can parse the files correctly.

inlineContent.description: Inline data rows embedded directly in the manifest, expressed either as a YAML array of objects or as a JSON array encoded as a string.

oneOf.description: Inline rows captured directly as a YAML/JSON array, for example: - {country: 'DE', cups: 10}.
Raw JSON array serialized as a multi-line string, for example '[{"country":"DE","cups":10}]'.

pattern.description: File path, directory, or glob pattern that selects one or more data files (for example './data/*.csv' or 'landing/sales/*.parquet').

dataSourceSpec.description: Configuration for a DataSource that collects raw data from files, inline content, or external databases and exposes it as a DuckDB view.

type.description: How this datasource loads data. Use 'inline' for embedded data, file types ('excel', 'csv', 'parquet') for file-based sources, or 'postgres_query'/'mysql_query' to run a SQL query against an external database.

path.description: File path, directory, glob pattern or URL used by file-based datasources. For example './data/landing/*.csv' or 's3://bucket/path/data.parquet'. Required for types 'excel', 'csv' and 'parquet'.

query.description: SQL query to execute against the remote PostgreSQL or MySQL database. Required for 'postgres_query' and 'mysql_query' types. The query result populates the DuckDB view (for example 'SELECT * FROM sales').

ephemeral.description: Controls whether this datasource is always refetched or may be cached between builds. When not set, bino derives ephemerality from the source type: database queries and remote URLs are treated as changing, inline content and in-project files as stable. Set explicitly to false to enable caching even for remote or database sources (for example a static S3 dataset).

sqlConnection.description: Connection parameters for 'postgres_query' and 'mysql_query' datasources. This block defines all non-credential connection details (host, port, database, user). Credentials are provided separately via a ConnectionSecret referenced by the 'secret' field.

host.description: Database server hostname or IP address (for example 'db.example.com').

port.description: Database server port number (for example 5432 for PostgreSQL or 3306 for MySQL).

database.description: Name of the database to connect to (for example 'analytics').

schema.description: Database schema name (optional, defaults to 'public' for PostgreSQL).

user.description: Username for database authentication. The password must be provided via the referenced ConnectionSecret.

secret.description: Name of a ConnectionSecret manifest (kind: ConnectionSecret) that contains database credentials (password only). The secret type must match the datasource type (postgres or mysql). When set, DuckDB uses the named secret for authentication.

connectionSecretSpec.description: Connection secret configuration for S3, HTTP, PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc. Database secrets (postgres, mysql) contain only credentials; connection details (host, port, database, user) are defined in the DataSource connection block.

scope.description: Optional file path prefix that the secret applies to (e.g. s3://my-bucket).

provider.description: Secret provider (defaults to 'config'). Use 'credential_chain' for automatic discovery.

postgresAuthSpec.description: PostgreSQL credentials. Connection details (host, port, database, user) must be defined in the DataSource connection block.

passwordFromEnv.description: Name of environment variable containing the database password (recommended over inline password).

mysqlAuthSpec.description: MySQL credentials. Connection details (host, port, database, user) must be defined in the DataSource connection block.

passwordFromEnv.description: Name of environment variable containing the database password (recommended over inline password).

keyIdFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the AWS access key ID.

secretFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the AWS secret access key.

sessionToken.description: AWS session token for temporary credentials.

sessionTokenFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the AWS session token.

endpoint.description: Custom S3 endpoint URL (for S3-compatible services).

keyIdFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the GCS access key ID.

secretFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the GCS secret access key.

usernameFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP username.

passwordFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP password.

bearerTokenFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP bearer token.

httpProxy.description: HTTP proxy URL (e.g. 'http://proxy.example.com:8080'). Used for datasource HTTP access. Note: The CLI respects the http_proxy environment variable only for DuckDB extension downloads; datasource access requires explicit proxy configuration via this field or httpProxyFromEnv.

httpProxyFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP proxy URL (e.g. 'http_proxy'). Takes precedence if httpProxy is not set.

httpProxyUsernameFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP proxy username.

httpProxyPasswordFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the HTTP proxy password.

keyIdFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the R2 access key ID.

secretFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the R2 secret access key.

connectionStringFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the Azure connection string.

accountKeyFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the Azure account key.

tokenFromEnv.description: Environment variable containing the Hugging Face token.

oneOf.description: Inline SQL query executed against DuckDB to produce this dataset.
Reference to an external SQL file containing the query.

query.description: SQL query executed against DuckDB to produce this dataset. Refer to DataSource names (their metadata.name) as tables or views, for example 'SELECT * FROM coffee_datasource'. Inline dependencies can be referenced via @inline(N) syntax where N is the zero-based index. Can be an inline string or a file reference using $file syntax. Mutually exclusive with 'prql' and 'source'.

oneOf.description: Inline PRQL query (Pipelined Relational Query Language) executed against DuckDB.
Reference to an external PRQL file containing the query.

prql.description: PRQL query (Pipelined Relational Query Language) executed against DuckDB. Can be an inline string or a file reference using $file syntax. The PRQL is compiled to SQL by DuckDB's prql extension. See https://prql-lang.org for syntax. Mutually exclusive with 'query' and 'source'.

source.description: Direct DataSource pass-through. When set, the DataSet becomes an alias for the referenced DataSource without transformation. Can be a string reference to a named DataSource or an inline DataSource definition. Mutually exclusive with 'query' and 'prql'.

dependencies.description: List of DataSource dependencies. Each item can be a string reference to a named DataSource or an inline DataSource definition. Inline DataSources are referenced in queries via @inline(N) syntax where N is the zero-based index.

$file.description: Relative path to an external file. The path is resolved relative to the manifest file containing this reference. Changes to the referenced file trigger cache invalidation and auto-reload in preview mode.

fileReference.description: Reference to an external file using the $file syntax. Supports SQL (.sql) and PRQL (.prql) files for queries.

dataSourceRefUnion.description: Reference to a DataSource by name OR an inline DataSource definition.

oneOf.description: Name of a DataSource document (metadata.name).
Inline DataSource definition.

inlineDataSourceSpec.description: Inline DataSource definition embedded directly in a DataSet or component. Same structure as dataSourceSpec but without requiring a separate document.

path.description: File path, directory, glob pattern or URL for file-based datasources.

query.description: SQL query for postgres_query and mysql_query types.

datasetRefUnion.description: Reference to a DataSet by name OR an inline DataSet definition.

oneOf.description: Name of a DataSet document (metadata.name).
Inline DataSet definition.

inlineDataSetSpec.description: Inline DataSet definition embedded directly in a component. Supports query, prql, or source fields.

query.description: SQL query. Inline dependencies can be referenced via @inline(N) syntax. Mutually exclusive with 'prql' and 'source'.

prql.description: PRQL query. Mutually exclusive with 'query' and 'source'.

source.description: Direct DataSource pass-through. Mutually exclusive with 'query' and 'prql'.

dependencies.description: DataSource dependencies. Inline DataSources are referenced via @inline(N) syntax.

format.description: Logical page size of the rendered artefact (for example 'xga', 'a4', 'letter').

orientation.description: Page orientation of the artefact, either 'portrait' or 'landscape'.

language.description: Language code passed to the renderer (currently 'de' or 'en').

filename.description: Output filename of the generated artefact (for example 'weekly-coffee-report.pdf').

title.description: Human-readable title of the artefact. Often reused in PDF metadata and UI.

description.description: Optional short description of the artefact contents, used in logs and metadata.

subject.description: Optional subject line stored in the PDF metadata.

author.description: Optional author or owning team name stored in the PDF metadata.

keywords.description: Optional list of keywords stored in PDF metadata to help search and classification (for example ['coffee', 'weekly', 'sales']).

signingProfile.description: Name of a SigningProfile manifest that should be used to digitally sign the generated PDF. If omitted, the artefact is not signed.

title.description: Human-readable title of the live report application.

description.description: Optional description of the live report application.

routes.description: Map of URL paths to route configurations. The root route '/' is mandatory and serves as the index page. Other routes map to ReportArtefact documents by name.

artefact.description: Name of the ReportArtefact document to render for this route (metadata.name). Cannot be used together with layoutPages.

oneOf.description: Name of a single LayoutPage document to render.
Array of LayoutPage document names to render in order.

layoutPages.description: One or more LayoutPage documents to render for this route. Cannot be used together with artefact.

title.description: Optional page title override for this route.

queryParams.description: Query parameters allowed for this route. These are substituted like ${VAR} environment variables in the report documents.

name.description: Name of the query parameter. Must be a valid identifier (used as ${name} in documents).

type.description: Type of the parameter. Determines the input control in the serve UI. Default is 'string'.

default.description: Default value if the query parameter is not provided. If omitted and optional is false, the request returns HTTP 400.

optional.description: If true, the parameter is optional even without a default value. The parameter will be empty/undefined if not provided.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this parameter controls.

options.description: Options for select type parameters. Either static items or a dataset reference.

items.description: Static list of options for select type.

dataset.description: Name of a DataSet to load options from.

valueColumn.description: Column name in the dataset to use as the option value.

labelColumn.description: Column name in the dataset to use as the option label. If omitted, valueColumn is used.

min.description: Minimum value for number or number_range types.

max.description: Maximum value for number or number_range types.

step.description: Step increment for number or number_range types.

value.description: The value to submit when this option is selected.

label.description: Human-readable label for this option. If omitted, value is used.

oneOf.description: Name of a single LayoutPage document to render.
Array of LayoutPage document names to render in order.

layoutPages.description: One or more LayoutPage documents to render. Components referenced in 'refs' must be present in these pages.

format.description: Logical viewport size for rendering (for example 'xga', 'hd', 'full_hd', '4k').

language.description: Language code passed to the renderer (currently 'de' or 'en').

filenamePrefix.description: Prefix for generated screenshot filenames (for example 'quarterly-report').

filenamePattern.description: Pattern for screenshot filenames: 'index' generates prefix-001.png, 'ref' generates prefix-refname.png.

imageFormat.description: Image format for screenshots, either 'png' or 'jpeg'.

quality.description: JPEG quality (1-100). Only applicable when imageFormat is 'jpeg'.

omitBackground.description: If true, captures screenshots with transparent background (PNG only).

scale.description: Screenshot scale: 'css' uses CSS pixels, 'device' uses device pixels.

refs.description: List of component references to capture screenshots of.

kind.description: Kind of the component to capture (for example 'ChartStructure', 'ChartTime', 'Table').

name.description: Name (metadata.name) of the component to capture.

datasetRef.description: Single or multiple dataset references. Each reference can be a string name or an inline DataSet definition.

oneOf.description: Name of a DataSet document (metadata.name).
Inline DataSet definition.
Array of DataSet references (string names or inline definitions).

content.description: Component style configuration expressed as an object or JSON string.

globalStyle.description: Global typography and color settings applied to all components.

fontSizePx.description: Base font size in pixels. Default: 13.3333

fontFamily.description: CSS font-family value. Default: 'Noto Sans', sans-serif

fontBackgroundColor.description: Background color for text elements (CSS color). Default: #ffffff

textStyle.description: Style settings for bn-text components. Currently extensible for future use.

timeChartStyle.description: Style settings for bn-chart-time (column/time series charts).

structureChartStyle.description: Style settings for bn-chart-structure (horizontal bar charts).

treeChartStyle.description: Style settings for bn-chart-tree (hierarchical tree diagrams).

edgeColor.description: Color for tree edges/connections (CSS color). Default: #404040

edgeWidth.description: Width of tree edges in pixels. Default: 1

operatorCircleRadius.description: Radius of operator node circles in pixels. Default: 10

operatorFontSizeScale.description: Scale factor for operator font size relative to global.fontSizePx. Default: 0.9

operatorColor.description: Color for operator nodes (CSS color). Default: #404040

scaleFactorsTimeChart.description: Scale factors for time chart element sizing (multiplied by global.fontSizePx).

scenarioOffset.description: Horizontal offset between scenario bars. Default: 0.3

scenarioLabelToColumn.description: Gap between scenario label and column. Default: 0.3

labelToColumn.description: Gap between category label and column. Default: 0.3

labelToAxis.description: Gap between label and axis. Default: 0.3

varianceAxisLineWidth.description: Width of variance axis lines. Default: 0.3

axisLineWidth.description: Width of main axis lines. Default: 0.1

scenarioLineWidth.description: Width of scenario indicator lines. Default: 0.2

markerWidth.description: Width of data point markers. Default: 0.7

markerOffset.description: Offset of markers from data points. Default: 0.0

axisTickLength.description: Length of axis tick marks. Default: 0.3

scaleFactorsStructureChart.description: Scale factors for structure chart element sizing (multiplied by global.fontSizePx).

scenarioOffset.description: Vertical offset between scenario bars. Default: 0.3

scenarioLabelToBar.description: Gap between scenario label and bar. Default: 0.1

labelToBar.description: Gap between category label and bar. Default: 0.3

labelToAxis.description: Gap between label and axis. Default: 0.3

varianceAxisLineWidth.description: Width of variance axis lines. Default: 0.3

axisLineWidth.description: Width of main axis lines. Default: 0.1

scenarioLineWidth.description: Width of scenario indicator lines. Default: 0.2

markerWidth.description: Width of data point markers. Default: 0.7

markerOffset.description: Offset of markers from data points. Default: 0.0

categoryWidth.description: Height allocated per category row. Default: 1.9

axisTickLength.description: Length of axis tick marks. Default: 0.3

svgStyleGroup.description: SVG styles grouped by scenario type (IBCS notation).

svgStyle.description: SVG styling properties for chart elements.

dasharray.description: SVG stroke-dasharray pattern (e.g., '10 5 5 5' for dashed lines).

fillpattern.description: Reference to a fill pattern (e.g., 'fc' for forecast hatching).

code.description: Locale code applied to the payload (e.g. de-DE).

namespace.description: Optional lookup namespace for the translation block.

content.description: Internationalization payload expressed as JSON or an inline object.

titleBusinessUnit.description: Name of the business unit displayed in card header. If empty, the business unit row is omitted.

titleDateFormat.description: Date format. year: 'YYYY', quarter: 'Q[Q] YYYY', month: 'MMM YYYY', week: '[KW]WW YYYY', day: 'DD.MM.YYYY', time: 'DD.MM.YYYY HH:mm:ss', none: 'DD.MM.YYYY', auto: automatic detection.

titleDateLink.description: Format in which the start and end date are linked. If empty the date row is omitted. avg: 'Ø[DATE]', interval: '[DATE]..[DATE2]', start: '.[DATE]', end: '[DATE].', ytd: '_[DATE]', ytg: '[DATE]_', cum: '[DATE]_[DATE2]', mat: '~[DATE]', none: '[DATE]'.

titleMeasures.description: Array of measure objects with name and unit. If all units are the same, redundant units will be reduced and names concatenated. If unit is empty, the row is omitted. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string. Example: [{"name": "Bruttoumsatz", "unit": "mEUR"}]

titleScenarios.description: List of scenarios displayed in card header. If empty, the scenarios are omitted. Values: ac, fc, py, pl.

titleVariances.description: List of variances calculated from the given scenarios. The scenarios used in the variances must also be defined in the scenarios property. Variance is defined as (ScenarioA - ScenarioB). The pos/neg/neu postfix defines which value is 'good' (positive or negative). If empty, variance charts are hidden. Pattern: d[scenarioB]_[scenarioA]_[pos/neg/neu].

titleOrder.description: Primary scenario on which the order direction is applied. If order is set to a scenario, it must also exist in the scenario list. If empty, the order arrow is omitted.

pageFormat.description: Defines the size of the page.

pageOrientation.description: Defines page orientation. Default is based on the given format.

messageImage.description: URL of the card's logo/pictogram. If not http, it looks up the URL in the component's asset folder. If empty, the image is omitted. Example: 'debug/concordia.png'.

messageText.description: Message text. If empty, the footer row is omitted.

layoutCardSpec.description: Configuration for a LayoutCard component. Cards are containers for child components (charts, tables, text) and support card-specific layout properties.

titleImage.description: URL of the card's title image. If not http, it looks up the URL in the component's asset folder. If empty, the image is omitted.

titleBusinessUnit.description: Name of the business unit displayed in card header. If empty, the business unit row is omitted.

titleScenarios.description: List of scenarios displayed in card header. If empty, the scenarios are omitted. Values: ac, fc, py, pl.

titleVariances.description: List of variances calculated from the given scenarios. The scenarios used in the variances must also be defined in the scenarios property. Pattern: d[scenarioB]_[scenarioA]_[pos/neg/neu].

titleOrder.description: Primary scenario on which the order direction is applied. If order is set to a scenario, it must also exist in the scenario list.

titleMeasures.description: Array of measure objects with name and unit. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string. Example: [{"name": "Bruttoumsatz", "unit": "mEUR"}]

titleDateFormat.description: Date format. year: 'YYYY', quarter: 'Q[Q] YYYY', month: 'MMM YYYY', week: '[KW]WW YYYY', day: 'DD.MM.YYYY', time: 'DD.MM.YYYY HH:mm:ss', none: 'DD.MM.YYYY', auto: automatic detection.

titleDateLink.description: Format in which the start and end date are linked. avg: 'Ø[DATE]', interval: '[DATE]..[DATE2]', start: '.[DATE]', end: '[DATE].', ytd: '_[DATE]', ytg: '[DATE]_', cum: '[DATE]_[DATE2]', mat: '~[DATE]', none: '[DATE]'.

footerText.description: Footer text displayed at the bottom of the card.

cardLayout.description: Layout mode for arranging child components within the card.

cardCustomTemplate.description: CSS grid-template-areas string for custom-template layout mode.

cardGridGap.description: Gap between grid cells (CSS gap value).

cardFitToContent.description: Whether the card should fit to its content size.

cardShowBorder.description: Whether to display the card border.

children.description: Child components to render inside the card slots.

kind.description: The type of component to render. Must match the kind of the referenced document when using ref.

metadata.description: Optional metadata for the child component, including name and constraints.

ref.description: Reference to a standalone document by name (metadata.name). When set, the referenced document's spec is used as the base, and any spec fields provided here act as overrides. The referenced document must have a kind matching this child's kind. LayoutPage cannot be referenced.

spec.description: Component specification. Required for inline children (without ref). When ref is set, spec fields override the referenced document's spec values.

imageSpec.description: Configuration for an Image component. Displays an image from an asset name or URL.

source.description: Name of an Asset document (kind: Asset) or URL of the image. If not http, it looks up the asset by name. If empty string ('') or null, the image is omitted.

layoutChildMetadata.description: Metadata for inline layout children (children of LayoutPage and LayoutCard).

name.description: Optional name for the inline child component. Used in error messages and for identification.

oneOf.description: String format constraint: 'field operator value', e.g., 'mode==preview', 'labels.env in [dev,staging]'

operator.description: Comparison operator: '==' (equals), '!=' (not equals), 'in' (value in array), 'not-in' (value not in array)

value.description: Value to compare: string or boolean for ==, !=; string array for in, not-in

oneOf.description: Structured format constraint with field, operator, and value

constraints.description: Constraint expressions that determine if this child is included for a specific ReportArtefact. Each constraint is evaluated against the target artefact's labels, spec, and the current mode (build/preview/serve). All constraints must match for inclusion. Supports both string format ('mode==preview', 'labels.env in [dev,staging]') and structured format ({field: 'mode', operator: '==', value: 'preview'}).

level.description: Determines the level on which the chart is aggregated.

order.description: Primary scenario on which the order direction is applied. If order is set to a scenario, it must also exist in the scenario list.

measureScale.description: Sets the SI prefix of measurements and converts the original value. '_' means no prefix. 'GREATEST' finds the largest value, 'LEAST' finds the smallest value in the dataset.

measureUnit.description: Measure unit displayed in the chart.

percentageScaling.description: Name of the scaling-group or a number representing the pixel per percentage.

unitScaling.description: Name of the scaling-group or a number representing the pixel per measure unit.

showMeasureScale.description: Toggles display of measure units in chart header.

limit.description: Maximum number of bars for each group. 0 means no limit. Rows cut by the limit are cumulated as REST.

scenarios.description: List of scenarios. Up to 4 different scenarios supported. Can be a YAML array or a JSON/CSV string. Values: ac1-4, fc1-4, pp1-4, pl1-4.

variances.description: List of variances calculated from the given scenarios. Pattern: d[scenarioB]_[scenarioA]_[pos/neg/neu]. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string.

axisLabelsMode.description: Axis labels mode. 'smart' only displays relevant information when a change/transition occurs (e.g., year displayed only on Jan 1st). 'long' and 'short' use respective date styles.

level.description: Determines the level on which the chart is aggregated.

order.description: Primary scenario on which the order direction is applied. If order is set to a scenario, it must also exist in the scenario list.

measureScale.description: Sets the SI prefix of measurements and converts the original value. '_' means no prefix. 'GREATEST' finds the largest value, 'LEAST' finds the smallest value in the dataset.

measureUnit.description: Measure unit displayed in the chart.

showMeasureScale.description: Toggles display of measure units in chart header.

limit.description: Maximum number of column stacks. 0 means no limit. Column stacks cut by the limit are cumulated as REST.

intervalSpanLimit.description: Max number of columns used to calculate axis date interval (if dateInterval is set to 'auto').

percentageScaling.description: Name of the scaling-group or a number representing the pixel per percentage.

unitScaling.description: Name of the scaling-group or a number representing the pixel per measure unit.

syncSpaceLeft.description: Left side offset for horizontal sync.

scenarios.description: List of scenarios. Up to 4 different scenarios supported. Can be a YAML array or a JSON/CSV string. Values: ac1-4, fc1-4, pp1-4, pl1-4.

variances.description: List of variances calculated from the given scenarios. Pattern: d[scenarioB]_[scenarioA]_[pos/neg/neu]. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string.

chartTreeSpec.description: Configuration for a ChartTree component. Tree charts display hierarchical structures with nodes connected by edges, commonly used for driver trees and decomposition diagrams.

edges.description: JSON array defining connections between nodes. Each edge specifies a source (from) and target (to) node ID, with optional operator and styling.

oneOf.description: JSON string containing array of edge objects.

operator.description: Mathematical operator displayed between sibling nodes.

label.description: Custom label to display instead of operator symbol.

oneOf.description: Array of edge objects defining the tree structure.

levelSpacing.description: Spacing between hierarchy levels in pixels.

nodeSpacing.description: Spacing between sibling nodes in pixels.

edgeStyle.description: Edge line style: 'straight' (direct lines), 'orthogonal' (right-angle lines), 'curved' (bezier curves).

showOperators.description: Show operator symbols between sibling nodes.

nodes.description: Array of node definitions with content for each node in the tree.

chartTreeNode.description: A node in the tree chart. Each node can contain a Label, Table, ChartStructure, or ChartTime component, either inline or by reference.

id.description: Unique identifier for the node, matching 'from' or 'to' values in edges.

kind.description: The type of component to render in this node.

ref.description: Reference to a standalone document by name (metadata.name). When set, the referenced document's spec is used. Only valid for Table, ChartStructure, ChartTime, and Image kinds.

spec.description: Component specification. Required for Label kind and inline definitions. When ref is set, spec fields override the referenced document's spec values.

chartTreeLabelSpec.description: Configuration for a Label component in a tree node. Labels display simple text or HTML content.

value.description: Text or HTML content to display in the label.

dataset.description: Optional dataset binding for template interpolation in the value.

gridSpec.description: Configuration for a Grid component. Grids create CSS grid-based layouts with row and column headers for organizing child components in a tabular structure.

chartTitle.description: Title displayed at the top-left of the grid. If empty, the title area is omitted.

rowHeaders.description: Row header definitions. Can be an array of strings (auto-generated numeric IDs) or objects with label and id properties.

oneOf.description: Simple array of row header labels. IDs are auto-generated as '0', '1', '2', etc.

label.description: Display text for the row header.

id.description: Unique identifier for the row, used in cell references.

oneOf.description: Array of row header objects with explicit IDs.

columnHeaders.description: Column header definitions. Can be an array of strings (auto-generated numeric IDs) or objects with label and id properties.

oneOf.description: Simple array of column header labels. IDs are auto-generated as '0', '1', '2', etc.

label.description: Display text for the column header.

id.description: Unique identifier for the column, used in cell references.

oneOf.description: Array of column header objects with explicit IDs.

showBorders.description: Whether to display border/divider lines between cells.

rowHeaderWidth.description: CSS width of the row header column (e.g., 'auto', '100px', '20%').

children.description: Array of child definitions. Each child specifies its grid position and content.

gridChild.description: A child (cell) in the grid. Each child specifies its position (row and column) and content, which can be a reference to an existing component or an inline definition.

row.description: Row identifier. Must match a row ID from rowHeaders (or auto-generated index like 0, 1). Can be string or integer.

column.description: Column identifier. Must match a column ID from columnHeaders (or auto-generated index like 0, 1). Can be string or integer.

kind.description: The type of component to render in this child.

metadata.description: Optional metadata for the child component, including name and constraints.

ref.description: Reference to a standalone document by name (metadata.name). When set, the referenced document's spec is used as the base.

optional.description: When true and the ref is missing, the child is skipped gracefully instead of erroring.

spec.description: Component specification. Required for inline children (without ref). When ref is set, spec fields override the referenced document's spec values.

tableTitle.description: A descriptive title for the table data.

order.description: Primary scenario on which the order direction is applied. If order is set to a scenario, it must also exist in the scenario list.

measureScale.description: Sets the SI prefix of measurements and converts the original value. '_' means no prefix. 'GREATEST' finds the largest value, 'LEAST' finds the smallest value in the dataset.

measureType.description: Sets the measure type of the table. Volume is displayed and interpreted differently than currency, and SI prefix names are adapted accordingly.

measureUnit.description: Measure unit displayed in the table.

categoryWidth.description: Width of the category column. 'inf' scales columns to the minimum space they need separately.

dataFormat.description: Defines the data unit type. If 'percent' is chosen, all data values are converted to percent (0.02 -> 2%).

dataFormatDigitsDecimal.description: Number of decimal digits in a table cell.

dataFormatDigitsPercent.description: Number of percent digits in a table cell.

grouped.description: Toggles whether data should be grouped by 'rowgroup'.

showGroupTitle.description: Toggles a title header for each row group. The title value is found by 'rowgroup'.

showMeasureScale.description: Toggles display of measure units in table header.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows for each group. 0 means no limit. Rows cut by the limit are cumulated as REST.

scenarios.description: List of scenarios displayed in the table. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string.

variances.description: List of variances calculated from the given scenarios. The scenarios used must be defined in the 'scenarios' property. Pattern: d[scenarioB]_[scenarioA]_[pos/neg/neu]. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string.

thereof.description: Array of drilldown objects with rowGroup, category, and subCategory. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string. Example: [{"rowGroup":"Umsatz", "category": "Applications", "subCategory": "Applications-A"}]

partof.description: Array of part-of objects with rowGroup and category defining the drill percentage location. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string. Example: [{"rowGroup":"Umsatz", "category": "Applications"}]

columnthereof.description: Defines which drill-column is displayed. Can be a YAML array or a JSON string. Example: [{"scenario": "ac1", "name": "DE", "subGroups": ["Berlin"]}]
